---
title: "Minimal Example Document"
author: "Your Name"
date: last-modified
---

## Introduction

This is a minimal example document for testing docstyle workflows. It demonstrates the essential elements needed for round-trip editing between Quarto and Microsoft Word.

The key principle of literate programming is that code and documentation should be woven together [@knuth1984].

## Key features

This document includes:

- Headings at multiple levels
- Body text paragraphs
- A bullet list
- Citations managed via Zotero

## List styles demonstration

### Simple bullet list

- First item
- Second item
- Third item

### Nested bullet list

- Level 1 item
  - Level 2 item
    - Level 3 item
  - Another level 2
- Back to level 1

### Simple numbered list

1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step

### Numbered list with bullet sub-items

1. Define the scope
   - Identify stakeholders
   - Review existing guidelines
   - Document assumptions
2. Gather evidence
   - Conduct literature review
   - Consult experts
3. Reach consensus
   - Run Delphi process
   - Hold final meeting

### Deeply nested numbered with bullets

1. Phase one
   - Task A
     - Sub-task i
     - Sub-task ii
   - Task B
2. Phase two
   - Task C
   - Task D
     - Sub-task iii

### Formal nested numbered list (1./a./i.)

This requires post-processing with `apply_list_style(docx_path, style = "formal")`:

1. First level item
   1. Second level item
      1. Third level item
      1. Another third level
   1. Another second level
1. Back to first level
   1. With sub-item

## Data visualisation

Effective communication of quantitative information requires careful attention to visual design [@tufte2018]. Clear typography and minimal chart-junk improve comprehension.

The semiotics of graphical representation provide a theoretical foundation for understanding how visual elements convey meaning [@bertin2011].

All three references [@knuth1984; @tufte2018; @bertin2011]

### A subsection

This subsection demonstrates level-3 headings.

## Table styles demonstration

### Formal table (top/bottom borders, shaded header)

::: {.table-formal}
| Domain | Responsibility |
|--------|----------------|
| Research | Design studies and analyse data |
| Policy | Advise on implementation strategies |
| Community | Provide lived experience perspectives |
:::

### Grid table (full borders)

::: {.table-grid}
| Version | Description | Date |
|---------|-------------|------|
| 1.0 | Initial release | 2025-01-15 |
| 1.1 | Bug fixes | 2025-02-01 |
| 2.0 | Major update | 2025-03-10 |
:::

### Plain table (Pandoc default)

| Item | Quantity | Price |
|------|----------|-------|
| Apples | 5 | $2.50 |
| Oranges | 3 | $1.80 |
| Bananas | 7 | $3.15 |

## Landscape section demonstration

Wide tables that don't fit in portrait orientation can be placed in a landscape section. Use `{{< pagebreak >}}` for the page break and `::: section-landscape :::` to change section properties.

{{< pagebreak >}}

::: section-landscape
:::

### Wide comparison table

| Category | Metric A | Metric B | Metric C | Metric D | Metric E | Metric F | Metric G | Status |
|----------|----------|----------|----------|----------|----------|----------|----------|--------|
| Group 1  | 12.5     | 23.8     | 45.2     | 67.9     | 89.1     | 34.6     | 56.3     | Active |
| Group 2  | 15.3     | 28.4     | 52.1     | 71.2     | 92.5     | 38.9     | 61.7     | Active |
| Group 3  | 18.7     | 31.2     | 58.9     | 75.6     | 95.8     | 42.3     | 66.2     | Review |
| Group 4  | 21.4     | 35.8     | 63.4     | 79.1     | 98.2     | 45.7     | 70.8     | Active |

{{< pagebreak >}}

::: section-default
:::

This text appears after the landscape section and should return to portrait orientation.

## References
